title: The One Where Chandler Doesn't Like Dogs

lines.text: Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Monica is in the kitchen, Joey, Rachel, and Phoebe are sitting around the table writing one notepads while Chandler is looking over their shoulders.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) Hey everybody! Happy Thanksgiving!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no, no. No-no-no.

lines.speaker: Joey, Rachel, and Phoebe
lines.text: Shhhh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, are we keeping Thanksgiving a secret this year?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, we're playing this game I learned at work. You have to name all the states in six minutes.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? That's like insanely easy!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Now, that's a lot harder than it sounds. You always forget at least one, or in some cases... fourteen (looks over to Monica).

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: It's a stupid game and I wasn't playing against other people, so technically I didn't lose.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? You forgot fourteen states?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Nobody cares about the Dakotas. (That's true in so many ways, trust me, I've lived in one and been to the other.)
[Chandler's watch beeps]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, okay, time's up!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, I got 48.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh that's not bad, Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I got tired of naming states. So I decided to list the types of celery, and I have one: regular celery.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, so Rachel's got 48 and Phoebe has the lead in...vegetables, Joey?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Say hello to the new champ of Chandler's dumb states game.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wow, how many have you got?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fifty-six!
Opening Credits
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's. Ross is sitting in the couch doing Chandler's game. Chandler is sitting in his barca-lounger. The girls are in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! How is New England not a state? Huh? They have a sports-team!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Does South Oregon have a sports-team? (Joey strikes one from his list) There you go.
[Cut to the girls in the kitchen.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (counting the place settings) How come we have one extra place setting?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: 'Cause you invited your assistant.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, right. Sorry. But Tag's not coming; his girlfriend came into town, so he's spending Thanksgiving with her.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh! Why didn't you tell me? I made him his own individual sweet potato stuffed pumpkin.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, I was going to, but then I figured, you know... you're food is so delicious and perfect, you can never have too many of those pumpkin things.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Now you think I wouldn't enjoy that, because it is so fake, (Laughs) but I still do.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Regular Celery! (Starts to write that on her list) Oh, I already have that. (She gets up and heads for her room)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Done! With time a-to-spare.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oooh that may be a New World's record (Looks at his watch and picks up Ross' pad)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know, I hate to lecture you guys, but it's kinda disgraceful, that a group of well-educated adults and Joey can't name all the states. Did you ever see a map, or one of those round, colorful things called "a globe?" Hmm?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, Magellan? You got 46 states. (Smiles and hands Ross back his pad)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What? That's impossible.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: 46. Wow! Who's well educated now, Mr. I-forgot-ten-states?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: All right, I'm out of oven space. I'm gonna turn on Joey's. Please, watch him! Do not let Joey eat any of the food!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I am only one man! (Monica heads out) Okay Ross, time is up!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, just give me another minute.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Look Ross, if you don't know them by now, you will never know them, okay? That is the beauty of this game. It makes you want to kill yourself.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: This-this is crazy! I can do this! All right, uhh, I bet I can get all 50 before dinner.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, but if you can't...no dinner!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You're on!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (gets up) All right. Don't look at my list, Ross, 'cause there's a lot on there that you don't have.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering, to Joey and Rachel) Hey, did you guys know, that your oven doesn't work?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: But the drawer full of take-out menus is okay, right?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, I'm gonna use yours, okay?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Pshhshhh!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler? Can you give me a hand? (Grabs her jacket)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure, and Joey; do not let Ross look at any of the maps or the globe in your apartment.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Don't worry, Chandler, it's not a globe of the United States.
[Chandler and Monica head out with some stuff. Phoebe comes out of her room with a bag.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey you guys I'm gonna go out and take a walk.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Phoebe, why is your bag moving?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, it's not!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Seriously, it's moving!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell is in there?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's just my knitting that's all! (A dog sticks its head out of Phoebe's bag. Everyone looks puzzled.) Yes! I knit this. I'm very good.
Ross' apartment, Monica and Chandler enter.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross's apartment is nice! How come we don't hang out here more often?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I don't know. Maybe it's because it smells a little weird. It's like old pumpkins or something.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's my pie!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Which smells delicious!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Uh-oh! Uh-oh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We left Joey alone with the food! (Walks towards the window and looks out) Yep! Yep, I knew it! There he is... feeding stuffing to a dog!
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, Ross is still doing his list. Rachel and Joey are feeding the dog as the phone rings.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (answering the phone) Hi Geller-Bing residence. How can I help?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, why is there a dog in our apartment?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm sorry, who's this?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, there's a dog sitting on my couch!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Tell her, I'm allergic, and I will sue!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, there's no dog here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes there is! He's black and white and shaggy and [Cut to Monica's apartment] he's sitting next to Rachel and licking Rachel's hand.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my god! Where are you? (Looks around.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll be right there!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Phoebe hangs up and someone knocks on the door. She gasps.) They're here already? How are they doing this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gets up and opens the door) Hi Tag! What are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I, uh, wanted to see if your offer to spend Thanksgiving with you is still good.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, sure! Come in! (He enters) Well, what-what happened to your girlfriend?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: We kinda broke up this morning.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, I'm sorry.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah, so she went back to Ohio.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohio!! Thank you!
[Time lapse, Chandler and Monica enter.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Huh! Where is the dog?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What dog? There-there's no dog here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah that dog left!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (walks to Phoebe's door) Phoebe! Phoebe, open up!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: There's no dog in here.
[The dog barks.]

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe, we can hear the dog barking!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No that's just me coughing! (Doing some weird coughing noises and the dog barks again. Phoebe comes out of the room.) Oh, good, there you are! Listen, um, I have a dog in my room.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is it doing here?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I'm watching it for some friends who went out of town. Wait. (She bends down, picks up the dog, and waves with one of its paws) Hello, my name is Clunkers. May I please stay with you nice people?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oooh, I wish she could stay here, but Chandler is allergic!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Extremely allergic, okay? If I'm anywhere near a dog for more than 5 minutes, my throat will just close up!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That's odd, 'cause this dog's been living here for the past 3 days

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (gasping) Really?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Chandler, if that dog's been here that long, and you haven't had a reaction, maybe you're not allergic to this dog?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it still has to go, right?

lines.speaker: Monica and Phoebe
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, it's um...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (interrupting him) Don't do it!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't do what?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) I have to! Okay? It's time! (Joey shrugs as if to say, "Do what you have to do") Okay, I hate dogs.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you crazy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are you out of your mind?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Told ya. (Waves bye-bye.)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They are needy, they are jumpy, and you can't tell what they are thinking, and that scares me a little bit.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Right, they are scary. (He jumps up, screaming) Ahh, she just ate a treat out of my hand!!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Wait a minute. Do you not like all dogs? I mean, not even puppies?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (scared) Is there a puppy here?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: You don't like puppies?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Tag) Okay, you are new!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Look, Chandler, I told you, never tell anyone about this dog thing. It's like Ross not likin' ice cream.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You don't like ice cream?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's too cold.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, it's just that dogs make me a little uncomfortable.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (to Phoebe) It hurts my teeth.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And I don't wanna say this, I don't you guys to hate me, but uh, I don't think, I can be around that dog anymore. Okay, so either the dog goes, or I go. (An awkward silence ensues.) Oh my god!!
[Time lapse, Ross is still doing Chandler's game. Tag is heading for the balcony.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How can I not get this? I'm a college professor; I got 1450 on my S.A.T.s.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Damn, I forgot you were here.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right. We're gonna take Clunkers to Ross's. We'll be back in a minute. (Gets up with Monica to do so.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, wait before you guys go, can I just ask you a question?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: When a guy breaks up with his girlfriend, what is an appropriate amount of time to wait before you make a move?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I'd say about a month.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Really? I'd say 3 to 4.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Half hour. (Rachel turns to look at him and he nods yes.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Interesting.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: When it's your assistant, I would say never.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, Rach, the big question is, does he like you? All right? Because if he doesn't like you, this is all a moo-point.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Huh. A moo-point?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, it's like a cow's opinion. It just doesn't matter. It's moo.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Monica and Phoebe) Have I been living with him for too long, or did that all just make sense?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Please, don't listen to Joey, okay. Would you look at him? He-he's obviously depressed. He's away from his family; he's spending Thanksgiving with strangers.   What he needs right now is for you to be his friend.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You're right, I'm sorry. Thank you. Okay, that's what I'm gonna do.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine! Take their advice. No one ever listens to me. When the package is this pretty, no one cares what's inside.
[Cut to the balcony, Tag is looking down while Rachel enters.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: How are you holding up?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Not bad.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah? I'm sorry about your girlfriend.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Thanks.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So were you guys together a long time?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: A year. On and off. I kinda thought we'd end up together. I don't anymore.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Now that she broke up with you?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. Hmmmm.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: It's weird. I always used to assume, that I would meet someone and fall in love and be happy and all that was just a given. But lately it's like what if it's not. Do you ever have that feeling?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No...Yeah, all the time, constantly. It's terrifying. But you know that I figure it...it has to work out.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Why?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Because, uh-it has to.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: You have all the answers, don't you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I know, I do. I really do.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Hey, thanks for talking to me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, what is a boss for? Hug it out! (They hug)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (through the window) All right, he likes you back! Huh? Told ya, you should go for it!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (realizes what he said) Street noise drowned any of that out? (Rachel moves madly towards him) No, all right, I see you later, okay... (Turns away embarrassed)
Commercial Break
The Balcony, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: What did Joey say? I like you back?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh, yeah, well, see, he... Joey knows, that I'm-I'm very insecure about my back and, and...you're hugging me, so obviously you are not repulsed by it, yeah!

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Wait-wait a minute; that doesn't make any sense.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No? (He shakes his head) All right, here's the truth um, Joey said what he said, because um, I'm attracted to you.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Wow. (He starts to walk towards the railing.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, I admit it. I have a crush on you, and uh, and, and I know that's crazy because we work together, and-and nothing could ever happen, and the last thing I want to do is-is to freak you out or make you feel uncomfortable. Which is why it would be really great if you said something right about now.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: (looking at the street) Oh my god! Those guys are stealing my car! (He points down to the street)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Right there! That's my car! (Sound of a breaking car-window) Hey!!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, that's gonna take them a minute. Do you have anything else you wanna get off your chest?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I can't believe this! (He walks back in again)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (she hurries after him) Wait, we still have time to talk and they're-they're not even in the car yet! (She takes a quick look down the street.) Oh look, there they go, okay. (She hurries in, too)
Ross' apartment, Monica and Phoebe sitting on the floor next to Clunkers basket.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay Phoebe, we should probably go back now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (doing Clunkers) Please don't leave me, I'll be lonely.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Stop it. Stop! Okay let's go. We can be strong.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, okay.
[They both get up and head for the door. Clunkers whines a little]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my god! Did you hear that? She said Monica! (She goes back to Clunkers again) Oooh, I can't leave her!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You know if you want, we can sneak the dog back in and Chandler wouldn't even know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's not gonna work.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I've had that dog there for three days and Chandler had no idea. He's not so smart.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey! I didn't know either.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, but you kinda knew that something was going on, didn't you?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, I knew.
Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's. Ross lays a lot off small papers, shaped like the U.S. states onto the floor making a map with the states. Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where's Chandler?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (comes out of the bathroom) Here I am.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wash your hands!!!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: How did you...know? (Heads back into the bathroom)
[Phoebe waves Monica in. Monica sneaks in with the bag with Clunkers in it and heads for Phoebe's room.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey! What's she doing back here?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Relax, Ross. She's not made of ice cream!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey look, Phoebe. I, uh, I laid out the states geographically...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (interrupts him) No, no, we don't really have time for this right now. Okay, we have to keep Chandler away from my bedroom.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, but, but look what I'm...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: See, this is exactly what we do not have time for. (She rushes into her room)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (comes out of the bathroom) Where's Monica?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Um, in Phoebe's room. You can't go in there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?
We can hear the dog whining at a high pitch.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Monica's crying. She's very upset about this whole Clunkers thing.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I, I should go in there.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No. No, no. She doesn't want to see you right now.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Because you sent away the dog!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is ridiculous. (He heads for Phoebe's bedroom)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, is it? Is it? Look, when Monica and I were kids, we had a dog named Rover. And, uh, one day, my dad decides, he doesn't like dogs. So Monica and her friend...Phyllis...take away the dog. And that was the last time we ever saw him. Don't you see? This is just like that. Only with a few details changed.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Okay, I'm in my sweat pants. Bring on the food! (Sees that Chandler has a worried look on his face) What's the matter?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Monica's all upset, because I sent Clunkers away.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So? Bring the dog back, you're a hero.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, I can be a hero, I could do that. I could, I could do... I, w-w-what if, what if it attacks me?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Chandler, it's like a big gerbil.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And that doesn't scare you? (He walks out)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross, you need some help?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: From you? (He does a weird desperate laughter, like he's almost crying) Yes, please!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (sits down next to Ross) First of all, Utah? Dude, you can't just make stuff up!
[Time lapse, Ross still laying a lot out the states.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I hate America! When I finish this game, I swear I am moving.
[Joey stands up again. Rachel enters the door]

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey! Tag's still talking to the police.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah, ohh! Why, damnit, why did I open my mouth? (In a girlish voice) I have a crush on you; I am attracted to you. (Back to normal again) Gee, I-I know that I freaked him out

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: If you said it like that, you probably did, yeah.
[Rachel walks past Joey towards the couch]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (opens her bedroom door and peeks out) Hey, is Chandler here?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No, no he went for a walk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, but you cannot tell him... but look whose back!
[The dog barks, runs out of Phoebe's room and jumps onto the couch]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (gasps) Hi!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh No-no-no-no-no-no-no! He went over to Ross' to bring the dog back here!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh no, the dog's not going to be there!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You think?
[The door opens and Chandler comes in. Rachel covers the door with a blanket]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi, honey.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Please, please, please, don't be mad at me.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What? Why, why would...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (interrupts her, to Monica) Shh, wait and see. Maybe we will, maybe we won't.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay, I went over to Ross' apartment to bring back Clunkers. Y'know, for you, and...(Clears his throat) I left the door open and she must have gotten out and I looked everywhere, all over the apartment, including the roof, which FYI Ross, one of your neighbors, growing weed. I couldn't find him, and I am so, so, so, sorry. But I do know where we could all go ease the pain. (Points up and then over to the street)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: We have good news, look whose back!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (uncovers the dog) Hi!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Clunkers?! Oh my god!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right, she came back all by herself.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's a Thanksgiving miracle!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Clunkers) It is so good to see you!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, she came all the way back from Ross' building. Oh, the things she must have seen! And then she climbed up the fire escape and she tapped on the window with her teeny little paw and then we ran to let her in...(Realizes, that Chandler starts to not believing her) I went to far, didn't I? When should I have stopped?
[Time lapse, after dinner. Ross stands up from his self-made map.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, maybe this is so hard, because there aren't 50 states. Let me tell you something, I have 49 states, and there are no more! I-I think, I should be able to eat something.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's up to you.
[Ross whines and starts working again. The door opens and Tag enters.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, hi! How are you doing?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: I'm okay. I gotta go down to the police station and look at mug shots.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Thanks for having me over, you guys.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Tag? Y-You're going? (Comes over to Tag) Uh we didn't, uh we didn't get the chance to talk. Uh, so, where did you say you're from again?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Colorado.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, what good are you. (Walks back to his map dejectedly.)
[Rachel and Tag go into the hall.]

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Look, um, I think we should talk about what happened on the terrace.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ah, I-I never should have said what I said. It-y'know what? It just doesn't matter how I feel. I mean we work together, so nothing could really ever happen between us, and what I would love is just to go to work on Monday, and-and never talk about this again, okay? Big day Monday lots to do. So, we're okay?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Um, I'm not.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, god, I know it, that I freaked you out.

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: No, you didn't. The only thing that freaked me out was you saying that nothing could ever happen between us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Tag
lines.text: Yeah, so, please don't fire me for doing this. (He kisses her)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, well, that's one less thing we have to do on Monday.
Ending Credits

Monica, Chandler, and Phoebe's, later that night, there is someone knocking on the door and Chandler stumbles out into the living room, turns on the light, looks through the peephole, and opens the door.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Delaware! (Starting to cry.) Delaware!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (hands Chandler his pad and walks in) I want my turkey now!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You got it. (Starts looking at the pad, while Ross got the turkey out of the fridge and starts to unwrap and it) You got Nevada twice.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pauses) I know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah. (Throws the pad on the table and heads for the bedroom)
End
